title: Creating a SB_LUT4 with unconnected inputs causes a C++ exception
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

Create the verilog file like;

```verilog
// 4-input, route-through LUT test.
module top( (* keep *) input [3:0] I, output O);
    //Cell instances
    SB_LUT4 #(
        .LUT_INIT(16'b0100000000000000)
    ) LUT  (
        .I0(),
        .I1(I[1]),
        .I2(),
        .I3(),
        .O(O)
    );
endmodule // top
```

Then run;

`yosys -p "synth_ice40 -nocarry; opt_clean; write_blif -attr -cname -param build-ice40-top-routing-virt-HX1K/lut.eblif" lut.v`

## Expected behavior

Not sure - probably a proper error message?

## Actual behavior

C++ exception shown below;
```
Generating RTLIL representation for module `\_90_lut'.
Successfully finished Verilog frontend.
No more expansions possible.

2.14. Executing ICE40_OPT pass (performing simple optimizations).

2.14.1. Running ICE40 specific optimizations.
terminate called after throwing an instance of 'std::out_of_range'
  what():  vector::_M_range_check: __n (which is 1) >= this->size() (which is 1)
```

Successfully finished Verilog frontend.
No more expansions possible.

2.14. Executing ICE40_OPT pass (performing simple optimizations).

terminate called after throwing an instance of 'std::out_of_range'
  what():  vector::_M_range_check: __n (which is 1) >= this->size() (which is 1)
